Paragraph: In recent years, superconductors without an inversion center, known as noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NSCs) have captured wide interests for their latent capacity of probing novel physical phenomena like unconventional superconductivity and topological excitations

In recent years, there has been a growing interest in noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NSCs), which are superconductors that lack an inversion center. These materials have shown potential for investigating unique physical phenomena such as unconventional superconductivity and topological excitations (Table 1).

Below is a paragraph from an academic paper. Polish and rewrite the writing to meet the academic style, improve the spelling, grammar, clarity, concision and overall readability. Paragraph: In recent years, superconductors without an inversion center, known as noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NSCs) have captured wide interests for their latent capacity of probing novel physical phenomena like unconventional superconductivity and topological excitations. In NSCs, the antisymmetric spin-orbit coupling (ASOC) effect can allow for the admixture of spin-singlet and spin-triplet pairing states. In this situation, some intriguing properties resulting from this unique Cooper pair formation such as nodal superconducting gaps, anisotropic upper critical fields that exceed the Pauli limit, and Majorana zero modes are expected to emerge. Moreover, this admixture pairing states transcends the typical Bardeen-Cooper-Schrieffer (BCS) like superconductivity and displays unique properties such as time-reversal symmetry (TRS) breaking in superconducting state and topologically protected nontrivial surface states.

In recent years, there has been a growing interest in noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NSCs), which are superconductors that lack an inversion center. These materials have captured attention due to their ability to explore intriguing physical phenomena, including unconventional superconductivity and topological excitations.

One significant effect observed in NSCs is the antisymmetric spin-orbit coupling (ASOC), which allows for the mixing of spin-singlet and spin-triplet pairing states. As a result, the formation of Cooper pairs in NSCs leads to fascinating properties such as nodal superconducting gaps, anisotropic upper critical fields that surpass the Pauli limit, and Majorana zero modes. These properties are anticipated to emerge as a consequence of this unique pairing state.

Moreover, the presence of these mixed pairing states goes beyond the conventional Bardeen-Cooper-Schrieffer (BCS) superconductivity, displaying distinctive characteristics such as time-reversal symmetry (TRS) breaking in the superconducting state and topologically protected nontrivial surface states.

Table 1: Modifications Made to the Paragraph

| Modification | Reason |
|--------------|--------|
| Use of more formal vocabulary | To meet the academic style |
| Addition of introductory phrase "There has been a growing interest" | To provide more context and clarity |
| Correction of spelling and grammar errors | To improve readability |
| Conversion of "wide interests" to "growing interest" | To enhance concision and clarity |
| Moved the explanation of NSCs to the beginning of the paragraph | To introduce the acronym and clarify its meaning |
| Rephrased the sentence about the ASOC effect | To improve clarity and concision |
| Added a sentence clarifying the unique properties resulting from Cooper pair formation | To provide additional information and clarity |
| Restructured the paragraph to enhance readability and logical flow | To improve the overall structure of the paragraph |

Below is a paragraph from an academic paper. Polish and rewrite the writing to meet the academic style, improve the spelling, grammar, clarity, concision and overall readability. Paragraph:  Among a series of synthesized NCSs up to now, CaPtAs was discovered as an exotic compound for simultaneously exhibiting TRS breaking and nodal superconductivity, making it a suitable candidate material to search and to construct novel electronic excitations like Majorana zero modes.

Among the various noncentrosymmetric superconductors (NCSs) synthesized to date, CaPtAs stands out as an intriguing compound. It displays the unique property of simultaneously breaking time-reversal symmetry (TRS) and exhibiting nodal superconductivity. This makes CaPtAs a promising candidate material for exploring and constructing novel electronic excitations, such as Majorana zero modes.

| Mentioned NCSs in brackets | To provide a clear definition of the acronym |
| Added explanation of TRS | To ensure clarity and understanding |
| Reordered the sentence to improve clarity and flow | To improve the overall structure of the paragraph |

Paragraph:  In so-called ‘conventional’ superconductors, additional symmetries are not broken in the superconducting state, and the properties are well described byBardeen–Cooper–Schrieffer (BCS) theory

In 'conventional' superconductors, there is no breaking of additional symmetries in the superconducting state, and the properties can be accurately described by the Bardeen–Cooper–Schrieffer (BCS) theory.

Below is a paragraph from an academic paper. Polish and rewrite the writing to meet the academic style, improve the spelling, grammar, clarity, concision and overall readability. Furthermore, list all modification and explain the reasons to do so in markdown table. Paragraph:  This review is concerned with SCs that break TRS, which leads to the appearance of spontaneous magnetic fields in the superconducting state—a particularly dramatic manifestation of unconventional pairing as all known SCs exhibit perfect diamagnetism via the Meissner effect, supporting a long-held view that magnetism and superconductivity are antagonistic states of matter

This review focuses on superconductors (SCs) that break time-reversal symmetry (TRS), resulting in the emergence of spontaneous magnetic fields in the superconducting state. This remarkable phenomenon represents an unconventional form of pairing, as all known superconductors exhibit perfect diamagnetism through the Meissner effect. This observation further supports the long-held notion that magnetism and superconductivity are mutually opposing states of matter.

Table 1: Modifications Made to the Paragraph

| Modification | Reason |
|--------------|--------|
| Use of more formal vocabulary | To meet the academic style |
| Correction of spelling and grammar errors | To improve readability |
| Added clarification of SCs acronym | To ensure clarity and understanding |
| Rephrased the sentence for clarity and concision | To enhance readability and understanding |
| Split the lengthy sentence into two shorter sentences | To improve digestion of the information |
| Rearranged the sentences for logical flow | To improve the overall structure of the paragraph |

Paragraph:  Taking advantage of recent progress on materials databases, in this paper, we obtain 56 compounds in total that share the same MgB2-like crystal structure after intensive structural screening, which started from ∼180 000 inorganic compounds from Atomly

Utilizing the advancements in materials databases, this study identifies a total of 56 compounds that possess the crystal structure similar to MgB2. The identification process involved intensive screening of approximately 180,000 inorganic compounds sourced from Atomly.

Below is a paragraph from an academic paper. Polish and rewrite the writing to meet the academic style, improve the spelling, grammar, clarity, concision and overall readability. Paragraph:  In order to evaluate the thermodynamic stabilities of these newly designed compounds, we first performed a high precision structure optimizations of these compounds, then the PhaseDiagram module from Python Materials Genomics (pymatgen) and Materials Project database were used to generate phase diagrams for each chemical system, and this allowed for the determination of the energy above the hull (Ehull) of every phases.

To assess the thermodynamic stabilities of the recently designed compounds, a two-step process was carried out. Initially, high-precision structure optimizations of these compounds were performed. Subsequently, phase diagrams for each chemical system were generated using the PhaseDiagram module from Python Materials Genomics (pymatgen) and the Materials Project database. This approach facilitated the determination of the energy above the hull (Ehull) for each phase.
